## Results

The waitlist-crossover model is a triangulation of the randomised ITT, not an
independent randomised experiment. Positive coefficients mean higher expected
post-score under the intervention-coded contrast.

In plain terms: this compares each child to themselves — the change while
waiting against the change after crossing over to the intervention — so every
child is their own control and stable child-level differences cancel out.

::: {.callout-warning title="Treatment-affected period-start score (graded DiD)"}
The **graded** waitlist-crossover models (`lrp-rli-did-001`–`009`) condition on the
period-start score through `gamma_own`. For the crossover arm's second period that
score is **post-treatment** (measured after its first on-intervention block), so
`gamma_own` adjusts a **treatment-affected baseline** (Rosenbaum 1984,
[doi:10.2307/2981697](https://doi.org/10.2307/2981697)) — a child random intercept
does not restore the total-effect reading. The parallel-trends assumption also
holds on the **logit-with-ceiling** scale here, not the raw-count scale. This is
the same concern the off-floor P/N models (DID-011/012) remove by dropping the
own-baseline term (#257); read the graded δ with it in mind.
:::

_How to read the contrast table:_ each row is one estimated quantity. `delta` is
the within-person difference-in-differences (DiD) treatment contrast: how much
larger the intervention-window change is than the waitlist-window change. The
`mean` (or `delta_mean`) column is the posterior average estimate; the `lo`/`hi`
(or `_lo`/`_hi`) columns bracket the 95% credible interval, the range the effect
plausibly falls in; and `p_pos` / `prob_delta_pos` is the probability the effect
is positive (a benefit). The `_items` columns restate `delta` on the raw items
scale so it reads as a count of items rather than logit units. For a heavily
floored outcome fitted with the off-floor rule (`P`, `N`), those columns instead
give a model-implied off-floor **risk difference** — the difference in the
probability of *being* off the floor at period end — because the estimand there is
off-floor prevalence, binary, not a graded count.

```{python}
# | echo: false
did_summary = _csv("did_summary.csv")
dose_slope_summary = _csv("dose_slope_summary.csv")
did_summary if did_summary is not None else dose_slope_summary
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import odds_string as _odds

if did_summary is not None:
    _d = did_summary.iloc[0]
    _off = "off_floor" in did_summary.columns and bool(_d["off_floor"])
    if "delta_mean" in did_summary.columns:
        _items = ""
        if "delta_items_mean" in did_summary.columns:
            if _off:
                _items = (
                    f", about a **{_d.delta_items_mean:+.3f} off-floor risk "
                    f"difference** (model-implied difference in Pr(off-floor at "
                    f"period end) from toggling Treated; 95% CrI "
                    f"{_d.delta_items_lo:+.3f} to {_d.delta_items_hi:+.3f})"
                )
            else:
                _items = (
                    f", about **{_d.delta_items_mean:+.1f} items** "
                    f"(95% CrI {_d.delta_items_lo:+.1f} to {_d.delta_items_hi:+.1f})"
                )
        print(
            f"**DiD treatment contrast (`delta`):** mean {_d.delta_mean:+.3f} "
            f"on the {'log-odds of being off the floor at period end' if _off else 'logit scale'} "
            f"(95% CrI {_d.delta_lo:+.3f} to {_d.delta_hi:+.3f}); "
            f"P(delta > 0) = {_d.prob_delta_pos:.3f} "
            f"({_odds(_d.prob_delta_pos)}).{_items}"
        )
    if "delta_mean" not in did_summary.columns and "beta_dose_mean" in did_summary.columns:
        print(
            f"**Session-dose association (`beta_dose`):** mean "
            f"{_d.beta_dose_mean:+.3f} logit units per 1 SD sessions "
            f"(95% CrI {_d.beta_dose_lo:+.3f} to {_d.beta_dose_hi:+.3f}); "
            f"P(> 0) = {_d.prob_beta_dose_pos:.3f}."
        )
if dose_slope_summary is not None:
    _main = dose_slope_summary.iloc[0]
    print(
        f"**Dose slope headline (`{_main.term}`):** mean {_main['mean']:+.3f} "
        f"(95% CrI {_main.lo:+.3f} to {_main.hi:+.3f}); "
        f"P(> 0) = {_main.p_pos:.3f}."
    )
```

::: {.callout-important title="Read dose terms as associations"}

Session dose is observational. A positive dose slope means higher session counts
are associated with larger conditional gains after the model's adjustment set; it
does **not** mean more sessions were randomised or proven causal.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
# Optional nested comparison copied next to this report by a comparison workflow.
_csv("did_dose_loo_compare.csv")
```
